title: Door in the Coral
synopsis: When a mysterious door appears in their coral reef home, Quokka must investigate this impossible addition to his underwater world while protecting the delicate ecosystem from potential harm.

pages.text: The sea anemones had counted wrong again. Quokka pressed his pink paws against the coral wall and stared at the wooden door that had definitely not been there yesterday. The thousand small fish swirled around him in silver ribbons, their scales catching the morning light that filtered down through the warm water. He could taste the salt and smell the sweet kelp forest nearby. The door stood exactly three coral formations tall, with brass hinges that somehow gleamed underwater. This was absolutely a problem that required serious attention.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin examining a wooden door embedded in bright coral formations, surrounded by swirling silver fish in clear blue water. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka examining a mysterious wooden door in the coral reef

pages.text: "Excuse me," Quokka said politely to a passing angelfish. "Did you notice when this door moved into the neighborhood?" The angelfish paused, her yellow fins fluttering. "Oh, that thing? It grew out of the orange brain coral around sunrise. Very strange installation, if you ask me." She swam away with the dignity of someone who had seen many impossible things. Quokka nodded thoughtfully. Growing doors were definitely not covered in the Reef Safety Manual.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin speaking with a yellow angelfish near orange brain coral formations, with the mysterious door visible in the background. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka talking to an angelfish about the mysterious door

pages.text: The little quokka swam closer to examine the door handle. It was made of what appeared to be polished seahorse shell, which was concerning because seahorses needed their shells. He made a mental note to check on the local seahorse population immediately after solving the door situation. The handle felt smooth under his paws and surprisingly warm. Behind him, a school of blue tangs formed an orderly queue, apparently waiting to see what would happen next.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin reaching toward a seahorse shell door handle while blue tangs line up behind him in the coral reef. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka reaching for the door handle with fish watching

pages.text: After four hours of careful observation, Quokka had determined that the door was definitely a door and definitely should not exist in his coral reef. The thousand small fish had grown bored and scattered to their afternoon feeding grounds, leaving only a few curious parrotfish to witness his next move. He had also noticed that the coral around the door seemed brighter and healthier, which was scientifically interesting but did not solve the fundamental door problem.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin floating in front of the wooden door with a few colorful parrotfish nearby, the coral around the door glowing more vibrantly. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka contemplating the door with parrotfish nearby

pages.text: "Well," Quokka announced to the parrotfish, "this calls for official door opening procedures." He straightened his small round ears and approached the handle with the seriousness the situation demanded. The brass hinges creaked as the door swung open, releasing a stream of the clearest, purest water Quokka had ever seen. It sparkled like liquid diamonds and carried the scent of new growth. The parrotfish gasped in unison, which was quite dramatic for fish.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin opening the wooden door as crystal-clear sparkling water flows out, with amazed parrotfish watching in the bright coral reef setting. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka opening the door with sparkling water flowing out

pages.text: Beyond the door lay what appeared to be an underwater garden filled with the most magnificent coral formations Quokka had ever seen. Towering elkhorn corals swayed gently, their branches reaching toward filtered sunbeams. Gardens of sea fans waved in perfect choreography, and tiny cleaner fish darted between anemones the size of dinner tables. "This is highly irregular," Quokka murmured, but he was already swimming through the doorway because irregular things required personal investigation.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin swimming through the doorway into a magnificent underwater garden with towering elkhorn corals, large sea anemones, and small cleaner fish. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka swimming into the underwater garden beyond the door

pages.text: Inside the coral garden, Quokka discovered something that made his permanent grin even wider. The water here was somehow cleaning itself, and tiny organisms were working together to filter out anything harmful. He watched a group of microscopic helpers repair a damaged piece of staghorn coral with the efficiency of a well-trained construction crew. "Ah," said Quokka to himself, "this is a coral rehabilitation center. Very sensible, really."

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin observing tiny organisms working on staghorn coral in the pristine underwater garden, with filtering systems visible in the water. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka watching tiny organisms repair coral in the garden

pages.text: A wise old grouper appeared from behind a massive brain coral and nodded at Quokka. "You must be the local reef guardian," the grouper said matter-of-factly. "We've been waiting for you to find us. This facility produces healing water for damaged reefs. We need someone responsible to help distribute it." Quokka considered this. Being appointed reef guardian was quite an honor, though he wished there had been some sort of ceremony or at least a badge.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin speaking with a large wise grouper near massive brain coral formations in the healing garden. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka meeting the wise grouper in the coral garden

pages.text: Quokka spent the rest of the afternoon learning about coral care, water purification, and the proper way to guide healing currents through a reef system. By evening, he had filled three large shells with the sparkling healing water and made a detailed plan for reef maintenance. As he swam back through the door, the thousand small fish were waiting for him with expressions of polite curiosity. "Routine maintenance situation," he reported. "Nothing to worry about." Though secretly, he was very pleased to have such important work.

illustration.description: A round smiling quokka with soft sandy-brown fur, small round ears, pink paws, and a permanent cheerful grin swimming back through the door carrying shells of sparkling water, with the thousand small fish gathered to greet him in the coral reef. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Quokka returning through the door with healing water for the reef

coverPrompt.alt: Quokka swimming near a coral reef with a mysterious door

questions.question: What did Quokka find growing in the coral reef?
questions.explanation: Quokka discovered a mysterious wooden door that had grown out of the orange brain coral overnight.

questions.question: What came out when Quokka opened the door?
questions.explanation: Crystal-clear, sparkling healing water flowed out when Quokka opened the door.

questions.question: What was behind the door?
questions.explanation: Beyond the door was a magnificent underwater garden filled with beautiful coral formations.

questions.question: Who told Quokka about the door's purpose?
questions.explanation: A wise old grouper explained that the door led to a coral rehabilitation center that produces healing water.

questions.question: What job did Quokka receive?
questions.explanation: The grouper appointed Quokka as the local reef guardian, responsible for distributing healing water to damaged reefs.
